subject: Directed Energy Weapons
executive_summary:
Directed energy weapons (DEW) — encompassing high-energy lasers (HEL) and high-power microwaves (HPM) — have crossed the threshold from experimental technology to operational deployment, with 2025-2026 marking the first confirmed combat uses of laser weapons in multiple theaters. Israel's Iron Beam system achieved the world's first combat intercepts using directed energy in October 2024 against Hezbollah UAVs, with the fully operational system deployed in December 2025 and integrated into the IDF by January 2026. By March 2026, Iron Beam had intercepted Hezbollah rockets in northern Israel, proving directed-energy interception is operationally viable in contested warzones. The U.S. Navy has deployed the ODIN (Optical Dazzling Interdictor, Navy) laser system on multiple Arleigh Burke-class destroyers during Operation Epic Fury, while the Army has deployed four Directed Energy M-SHORAD (50-kilowatt laser on Stryker) systems to the U.S. Central Command area of operations.

The U.S. Department of Defense spends approximately $1 billion annually on directed energy development, with FY2025 requesting $789.7 million for unclassified DE programs. The Army's Enduring High Energy Laser (E-HEL) program is positioned to become the first DE program of record, with HII's new production facility expected to begin low-rate initial production by mid-2026 and a competitive source selection planned for Q2 FY2026. Key contractors include Raytheon/RTX, Lockheed Martin, Northrop Grumman, Boeing, and Rafael (Israel). The technology is maturing rapidly for counter-UAS and short-range air defense missions, with the Army describing laser weapons as 'pretty mature' and ready for production scaling.

China has aggressively entered the directed energy competition. At its September 2025 military parade, Beijing unveiled the LY-1 high-power laser weapon, the OW5-A50 Laser system, and the 'Hurricane 3000' high-power microwave system capable of disabling dozens of drones in a single blast. Chinese scientists have tested a compact HPM gun capable of firing over 10,000 shots without failure. Most strategically concerning, China is developing HPM systems described as 'Starlink killers' designed to disable satellite constellation nodes, and the PLA views electromagnetic warfare as a 'system versus system' first-wave non-kinetic strike capability targeting command centers, radar, missile defense, and communications networks.

key_findings.headline: Israel's Iron Beam Achieves First-Ever Combat Laser Intercepts
key_findings.detail: Israel's Iron Beam (Or Eitan/Strong Light) system achieved the world's first combat intercepts using directed energy in October 2024 against 40 Hezbollah UAVs during the Gaza War. The fully operational 100-kilowatt system was deployed in December 2025 and integrated into the IDF by January 2026. By March 2026, Iron Beam intercepted Hezbollah rockets in northern Israel, proving directed-energy interception under real combat conditions in a contested warzone. The system dramatically reduces per-shot costs compared to traditional missile interceptors.

key_findings.headline: U.S. Army Deploys DE M-SHORAD to CENTCOM — First Operational Laser Weapon Use
key_findings.detail: The U.S. Army has deployed four Directed Energy M-SHORAD systems (50-kilowatt laser on Stryker vehicle) to the U.S. Central Command area of operations, marking the first operational use of U.S. Army laser weapons. A total of 11 DE M-SHORAD systems have been produced. The Army's E-HEL program is on track to become the first directed energy program of record, with HII's production facility expected to begin low-rate initial production by mid-2026.

key_findings.headline: China Unveils LY-1 Laser and 'Hurricane 3000' Microwave Weapon at Military Parade
key_findings.detail: At China's September 2025 military parade, Beijing displayed the LY-1 high-power laser weapon for shipboard and land-based defense, the OW5-A50 Laser HMV3 system, and the 'Hurricane 3000' high-power microwave weapon capable of disabling dozens of drones in a single blast. Chinese scientists have also tested a compact HPM gun achieving over 10,000 shots without failure or performance degradation, a breakthrough documented in peer-reviewed research.

key_findings.headline: China Developing HPM 'Starlink Killer' Anti-Satellite Weapons
key_findings.detail: China is developing high-power microwave weapons specifically designed to disable satellite constellation nodes, described as 'Starlink killers.' The PLA doctrine treats electromagnetic combat as 'system versus system' warfare, employing HPM weapons as first-wave non-kinetic strike tools to disable high-value electronic targets including command centers, radar installations, missile defense systems, and communications networks before kinetic engagement.

key_findings.headline: U.S. Navy ODIN Laser Deployed on Destroyers During Operation Epic Fury
key_findings.detail: The U.S. Navy has equipped multiple Arleigh Burke-class destroyers with the ODIN (Optical Dazzling Interdictor, Navy) laser system, with confirmed operational deployment during Operation Epic Fury. ODIN is part of a broader directed-energy roadmap that includes more powerful high-energy laser initiatives intended to counter cruise missiles. Combat footage confirmed the linkage of a live combat strike with the Navy's shipboard laser system.

key_findings.headline: E-HEL Program to Become First U.S. Directed Energy Program of Record
key_findings.detail: The Army's Enduring High Energy Laser (E-HEL) program is positioned to become the first directed energy program of record in the U.S. military. The Army issued an RFI in October 2025 to inform competitive source selection planned for Q2 FY2026. HII's new facility is expected to begin low-rate initial production activities by mid-2026, transitioning DE weapons from prototype to sustained production.

key_findings.headline: DoD Directed Energy Spending at $1 Billion Annually but Declining Budget Requests
key_findings.detail: The Department of Defense spends approximately $1 billion annually on directed energy weapon development, but the FY2025 request of $789.7 million was down from the $962.4 million request and $1.1 billion appropriation in FY2024. This declining budget trajectory contrasts with the technology's demonstrated operational success and the growing threat from Chinese and Russian DE programs. A GAO report recommended DOD focus on transition planning to move DE weapons from development to production.

recent_developments.headline: Iron Beam Intercepts Hezbollah Rockets in Northern Israel
recent_developments.summary: Israel's fully operational Or Eitan (Iron Beam) system intercepted Hezbollah rockets fired at northern Israel, confirming the system's capability against rocket threats in addition to UAVs. This marked the third confirmed combat engagement for directed-energy interception, following October 2024 UAV intercepts and the December 2025 deployment. The system has now proven effective against both low-slow UAVs and faster rocket threats.
recent_developments.impact: Validates directed-energy interception against diverse threat types in combat, likely accelerating international procurement and deployment timelines.

recent_developments.headline: Israel Fields Operational Iron Beam System; U.S. Army Deploys DE M-SHORAD to CENTCOM
recent_developments.summary: Two milestone deployments occurred simultaneously: Israel fielded its fully operational 100-kilowatt Iron Beam laser system in December 2025, and the U.S. Army deployed four DE M-SHORAD 50-kilowatt laser Stryker systems to the CENTCOM area of operations. Both deployments marked the transition of directed energy from experimental to operational military capability, with Israel and the U.S. becoming the first nations to field combat-ready laser weapons.
recent_developments.impact: Establishes the directed energy era in military operations, with two allied nations simultaneously fielding operational laser weapons.

recent_developments.headline: China Displays Three Directed Energy Systems at Military Parade
recent_developments.summary: China's September 2025 military parade featured the LY-1 high-power laser weapon, the OW5-A50 Laser HMV3 vehicle-mounted system, and the Hurricane 3000 high-power microwave weapon. The HPM system was described as capable of disabling dozens of drones in a single blast. These displays demonstrated China's parallel development of both laser and microwave directed energy capabilities across land and naval domains.
recent_developments.impact: Reveals China's breadth of DE development across laser and HPM categories, suggesting a multi-domain directed energy strategy that complements kinetic systems.

recent_developments.headline: China's 'Starlink Killer' HPM Weapons Reach New Development Stage
recent_developments.summary: Reports emerged of China's advancing development of high-power microwave weapons specifically designed to disable satellite constellation nodes, described as 'Starlink killers.' These systems represent a counter-space capability that could neutralize the satellite communications and ISR infrastructure that modern Western militaries depend upon, without creating debris that kinetic ASAT weapons produce.
recent_developments.impact: Poses a direct threat to the space-based communications, navigation, and ISR infrastructure that underpins U.S. and allied military operations.

recent_developments.headline: U.S. Army Issues RFI for Enduring High Energy Laser Production
recent_developments.summary: The Army issued a request for information on October 30, 2025 to inform its Enduring High Energy Laser production effort, with competitive source selection planned for Q2 FY2026. The E-HEL is slated to become the first directed energy program of record, setting the path for full-scale deployment. HII invested in a new facility expected to begin low-rate initial production by mid-2026.
recent_developments.impact: Marks the transition from DE prototyping to a formal program of record, establishing the industrial base and procurement framework for scaled production.

risk_assessment.current_risk: Directed energy weapons have transitioned from experimental to operational status, with both the U.S. and Israel fielding combat-ready laser systems. However, a significant risk is emerging from China's parallel development of both high-energy laser and high-power microwave capabilities, particularly HPM weapons designed to disable satellite constellations. The U.S. faces a budgetary disconnect: while the technology is described as 'pretty mature' and combat-proven, DE spending requests are declining ($789.7M in FY2025 vs $1.1B appropriated in FY2024). The counter-UAS application has been validated in combat, but scaling to higher-power systems capable of defeating cruise missiles and addressing China's HPM threat to space assets requires sustained investment that current budget trends do not support.
key_drivers: Israel's Iron Beam proving combat viability of laser interception against UAVs and rockets
U.S. Army DE M-SHORAD operational deployment to CENTCOM confirming readiness
China's HPM 'Starlink killer' development threatening space-based infrastructure
E-HEL becoming first U.S. DE program of record, establishing production framework
Declining DoD DE budget requests despite technology maturation and combat success
Counter-UAS demand surge from drone proliferation across all theaters
Cost-per-shot advantage of lasers over traditional missile interceptors
mitigation_actions: Increase DE funding to match technology maturity and operational demand, reversing declining budget trend
Accelerate E-HEL program of record to achieve full-rate production by 2028
Develop counter-HPM hardening for satellite constellations and critical space assets
Invest in scaling laser power from 50-100 kW counter-UAS to 300+ kW counter-cruise missile capability
Expand U.S.-Israel cooperation on combat-proven directed energy technology
Establish domestic DE industrial base through competitive source selection for E-HEL
Develop shipboard high-energy laser systems capable of cruise missile defense
Fund space-based DE research for both offensive and defensive applications
